channel.title: Crime-Justice on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Crime-Justice on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Former Special Operator Who Neutralised Eight Home Invaders Convicted After Failing To Wait Until Shot Politely Enough
item.description:
BRISBANE — A former special operator has been convicted of second-degree murder after defending his family from eight armed home invaders in what prosecutors described as “an excessive and worryingly competent response to a challenging interpersonal incident”.

The court heard that Damien Redfern, 42, a former special forces soldier, defensive tactics instructor, private security consultant, emergency-response trainer, and the only man in southern Queensland known to have reverse-engineered a household mop into a non-lethal perimeter deterrent, responded to the break-in “too quickly, too decisively, and with insufficient visible helplessness”.

item.title: Victorian Government Confirms Machete Ban Remains Effective Against Citizens Already Unlikely To Bring A Machete To A Fight
item.description:
MELBOURNE — The Victorian Government has confirmed its machete ban remains highly effective against all citizens who were already unlikely to bring a machete to a fight, despite reports that more than 40 public knife fights have occurred in the year since the policy was announced.

Officials defended the ban after critics described it as an “absolute failure”, saying the policy had successfully prevented thousands of accountants, nurses, retirees, office workers, parents, librarians and people waiting quietly for trams from carrying machetes in public.
